how-ham-radio-repeaters-work.md.title: How Ham Radio Repeaters Work
how-ham-radio-repeaters-work.md.summary: Why a 5-watt handheld can suddenly talk across three counties: the duplex offset, CTCSS gatekeeping, duplexer cavities, and mountaintop etiquette behind an amateur repeater, traced through the W4TRC machine on Bays Mountain.

why-my-zip-code-not-my-employers-decides.title: Why My Zip Code — Not My Employer's — Decides Whether I Get Paid Overtime
why-my-zip-code-not-my-employers-decides.summary: A fiscal year of timesheets turned up 551 unpaid overtime hours — tracing why led through FLSA exempt-duties tests, why the employee's state (not the employer's HQ) governs, and why claiming California residency on paper wouldn't fix it.

tabnext.md.title: tabnext — Open New Browser Tabs Next to Your Current One
tabnext.md.summary: When the MV2 purge killed Open Tabs Next to Current, rebuilding it took 105 lines and zero permissions — plus two non-obvious lessons: foreground tabs are already active when onCreated fires, and a session restore looks exactly like a user opening forty tabs, so naive tab-moving rips them out of their groups.

raccoons-are-illegal.md.title: Raccoons Are Illegal
raccoons-are-illegal.md.summary: Rescuing two orphaned raccoon kits — Charls and Doozy — turned into a two-hour race against the bureaucracy of compassion. What Tennessee's Rabies Vector Species laws do to anyone who tries to help, and the underground network of rehabbers who actually will.

my-cognitive-and-longevity-stack.md.title: My Cognitive & Longevity Stack
my-cognitive-and-longevity-stack.md.summary: A software engineer's morning-to-night health stack, framed as a build/runtime/shutdown cycle — why a dawn run and sunlight anchor the cortisol curve, how the D3→K2→magnesium→omega-3 pieces interlock, and why the timing is the real intervention. A log, not a prescription.

use-the-best-model.md.title: Use the Best Model. Your Time Is the Expensive Part.
use-the-best-model.md.summary: The economic case for defaulting to the best frontier model on interactive engineering work — the model is cheap, your hours aren't — plus the five specific situations where stepping down is the right call.

bring-back-the-hit-counter.md.title: Bring Back the Hit Counter — Privately, in One File
bring-back-the-hit-counter.md.summary: A privacy-respecting hit counter in a single file of PHP — counts each visitor once per day with salted IP hashes, no database and no tracking, and you can drop it straight into your own site.

cron-assumes-your-computer-never-sleeps..title: Cron Assumes Your Computer Never Sleeps
cron-assumes-your-computer-never-sleeps..summary: I'd run cron for a decade — on servers, where machines never sleep. Then I gave it a job on my laptop and it silently never ran. anacron is cron for computers that power off: it thinks in elapsed days, catches up after downtime, and (the part nobody mentions) rides on top of cron rather than replacing it.

paste-markdown-into-medium.md.title: One Command to Paste Markdown Into Medium
paste-markdown-into-medium.md.summary: Medium closed its API — but its editor still honors the text/html clipboard flavor. So I built mdpaste, a single Rust binary that turns markdown into paste-ready rich text for Medium, Substack, and Ghost.

the-terminal-setup-i-cant-work-without.m.title: The Terminal Setup I Built and Can't Work Without
the-terminal-setup-i-cant-work-without.m.summary: I patched suckless `tabbed` into a terminal I actually love: clickable, tmux-keyed tabs of real terminals — transparent, pywal-themed, and self-naming. Rename a Claude Code session and the tab follows live. One `yay` on any Arch box.

how-my-links-got-their-pictures.md.title: How My Links Got Their Pictures
how-my-links-got-their-pictures.md.summary: The Open Graph protocol gives every shared link its title, blurb, and picture — and three traps I hit making it work on a hash-routed single-page app: the fragment that never reaches the server, the blur, and the cache that never forgets.

claude-code-permission-modes.md.title: Why Claude Code Keeps Asking for Permission (and How to Make It Stop)
claude-code-permission-modes.md.summary: Permission mode is fixed at startup, so resumed sessions silently reset to prompting — and Shift+Tab deliberately won't cycle to bypass. The one-line settings.json fix, and what to do with sessions already running.

claude-tips-complete-list.md.title: Claude /tips: The Complete List
claude-tips-complete-list.md.summary: Every tip Claude Code ships with — extracted by pointing Claude at its own binary — plus the keyboard shortcuts and sigils that aren't in the rotation.

osi-model-whiteboard.md.title: The OSI Model, Explained from a Whiteboard
osi-model-whiteboard.md.summary: A whiteboard walk-through of the seven layers — what each one actually does, and why “that's a Layer 7 problem” still means something even though the modern internet runs on TCP/IP.

pipeline-vs-harness.md.title: Pipeline vs Harness
pipeline-vs-harness.md.summary: Two words that sound alike and get used loosely but mean opposite things — a pipeline is a conveyor belt that work flows through; a harness is a jig that wraps a fixed thing to drive it. The distinction, with the instances we actually use.

how-to-config-obsidian.md.title: How to Configure Obsidian the Agentic Way

dependency-hell.md.title: Dependency Hell: Why Software Breaks When Everything Works

custom-claude-skills.md.title: How to Write a Custom Claude Code Skill

claude-code-agent-teams.md.title: Claude Code Agent Teams: Put Your AI Coworkers in tmux Panes

trng-deep-dive.md.title: True Random Number Generators: From Quantum Physics to Silicon

install-signal-on-aegix.md.title: Install Signal Desktop on Aegix Linux

add-articles.md.title: How to Add New Articles to timbeach.com

how-my-blog-got-a-voice.md.title: How My Blog Got a Voice
how-my-blog-got-a-voice.md.summary: Adding read-aloud to this blog took three tries — Web Speech that stays silent on Linux, in-browser WASM that hung the page — before the dumb-in-retrospect fix: render the audio once at publish time and ship the bytes. Plus the two parser bugs hiding on the way.

aegix-on-the-aur.md.title: From ~/.local/src to the AUR
aegix-on-the-aur.md.summary: How my suckless edits travel from ~/.local/src into the AEGIX monorepo and out to the AUR as installable -git packages — a post-commit hook, four packages, and one rule: live on this machine is truth, the network is a manual gesture.

circleback.md.title: Future Me Has Receipts: Building circleback
circleback.md.summary: A dozen long-running Claude Code sessions across unrelated projects — and the hard part isn't building the work, it's finding your way back to each thread.

rss-still-really-simple.md.title: Still Really Simple: A Short History of RSS (and How This Site Uses It)
rss-still-really-simple.md.summary: A short history of RSS — what it is, why it still matters, and exactly how this static site builds the feed at /feed.xml.

change-your-luks-passphrase.md.title: How to Change Your LUKS Passphrase (and Other Sysadmin-y Things)
change-your-luks-passphrase.md.summary: The add-verify-kill sequence that changes a LUKS passphrase with zero lockout risk, why usermod refuses to rename a logged-in user (and the detached-script trick around it), and the stale /etc references a rename leaves behind.

three-coming-soon-sites-on-one-vps.md.title: Three Coming-Soon Sites on One VPS: nginx, Let's Encrypt, and a Repo Each
three-coming-soon-sites-on-one-vps.md.summary: A repeatable, verify-as-you-go routine for taking three domains from fresh DNS to HTTPS-with-auto-renewing-certs and a private repo each — on one VPS, in about twenty minutes.

your-backup-is-a-hypothesis.md.title: Your Backup Is a Hypothesis
your-backup-is-a-hypothesis.md.summary: Why a backup you haven't booted is a hypothesis, not a backup — and how aegix-backup turns a dead encrypted laptop drive into a five-minute recovery on Aegix/Artix with nightly re-earned bootable clones and a ciphertext-only offsite.
